  - SERVICE_RESALE_ITEM
  - TIME_SHEET
  - TIME_BILL

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In NetSuite Connector, connections using lowercase values in the <code>Account ID</code> field no longer fail.</p></td><td><p>W-11469671</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## August 3, 2022

### Whatâs New

- You can now connect a new system: MuleSoft Robotic Process Automation (RPA).

  See [the product documentation](ms_composer_reference) for details about each connector.

## August 2, 2022

### Whatâs New

- In Salesforce Connector, you can now specify conditions for triggers.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Labels are now properly capitalized in ServiceNow Connector.</p></td><td><p>W-10575461</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## July 13, 2022

### Whatâs New

- When deleting a connection, a list of active and inactive flows using the selected connection now
  appears. Using this list, if the connection is:
  - Not used in any flows, you can delete the connection.
  - Used in inactive flows, a list containing the name and count of inactive flows using this
    connection appears. Deleting this connection will affect the listed flows. If you reopen the
    inactive flow, you must select a new connection.
  - Used in active and inactive flows, a list containing the name, count, and status of active and
    inactive flows using this connection appears. If you choose to delete the connection, secondary
    confirmation appears. Prior to deleting the connection, Composer will first attempt to stop the
    flows.

## June 28, 2022

### Whatâs New

- In NetSuite Connector and Salesforce Connector, empty field values are ignored by default. To
  clear empty values, you can now select the `Clear on Null Values` option.

## June 6, 2022

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Workday Connector, the connection validation now succeeds even if the user does not have access to the <code>Performance_Management</code> service.</p></td><td><p>W-11193505</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fixed reported security vulnerabilities in Workday Connector by upgrading the Spring Framework library from <code>org.springframework:spring-context:5.3.18</code> to <code>org.springframework:spring-context:5.3.20</code>.</p></td><td><p>W-11195299</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## May 31, 2022

### Whatâs New

- In NetSuite Connector, empty field values are ignored by default. To clear empty values, you can
  now select the `Clear on Null Values` option.

## May 20, 2022

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fixed the issue in Jira Connector in which a blank value in the <strong>Description</strong> field incorrectly returned an invalid input error.</p></td><td><p>W-11107531</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## May 17, 2022

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fixed the issue in Slack Connector in which a null pointer exception occurred after cleaning up markup in a plain text string.</p></td><td><p>W-11031313</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## May 11, 2022

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>ServiceNow Connector now loads metadata for all WebService accessible tables automatically.</p></td><td><p>W-10919969</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>In ServiceNow Connector, patched security vulnerability in dependency <code>org.springframework : spring-beans : 5.3.17</code>.</p></td><td><p>W-10981860</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>In ServiceNow Connector, patched security vulnerability in dependency <code>org.springframework : spring-context : 5.2.7.RELEASE</code>.</p></td><td><p>W-11001899</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## May 5, 2022

### Whatâs New

- The following objects are now available for NetSuite Connector:
  - Service Item for Sale
  - Service Item for Purchase
  - Return Authorization

Note that the objects will appear in all caps and with underscores.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fixed the null pointer exception issue that occurred in NetSuite Connector using a trigger that had been previously modified to select a custom object.</p></td><td><p>W-10995359</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## April 29, 2022

### Whatâs New

The following actions were added to Workday Connector to enable Workday Financial Management:

- **Create Object**
- **Update Object**
- **Get Object**

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fixed the issue in Workday Connector in which you could not filter fields containing values with the file data type.</p></td><td><p>W-10574439 COCN-667</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>In Workday Connector, when editing a <strong>Job Requisition</strong> action, the <strong>Position Time Type</strong> field is now selectable as a list or set as a data pill.</p></td><td><p>W-10574489 COCN-605</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>When a generic error message is returned from the API in Workday Connector, the error message that occurs in Composer is now informative.</p></td><td><p>W-10574413 COCN-705</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Improved validation error messages in Workday Connector to make them more specific.</p></td><td><p>W-10574467 COCN-632</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>In Workday Connector, the <strong>Position Title</strong>, <strong>Supervisory Organization</strong>, and <strong>Location</strong> fields are now optional in the <strong>Create Worker</strong> action. However, if the <strong>Location</strong> value is not set on the <strong>Position</strong> object, then the <strong>Location</strong> field is required, or the Create Worker API operation will fail.â</p></td><td><p>W-10574483 COCN-612</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>In Jira Connector, pagination is now enabled to fetch all assignable users in the <strong>Reporter</strong> and <strong>Assignee</strong> fields when using the <strong>Create issue with custom fields</strong> action.</p></td><td><p>W-10934109</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## April 28, 2022

### Whatâs New

- You can now connect a new system: HTTP.

  See [the product documentation](ms_composer_reference) for details about each connector.

## April 26, 2022

### Whatâs New

- When enabled, checks run every 15 minutes on running flows. If any flow fails, Composer sends an
  email to the owner of the flow.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In the JIRA Connector, entering a string value containing quotes in the <strong>Description</strong> field no longer causes an invalid JSON parsing request.</p></td><td><p>W-11037529</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## April 22, 2022

### Whatâs New

- In the Google Sheets and Slack Connectors, you can now hover over certain triggers, actions, and
  fields to view in-app contextual help.

## April 18, 2022

### Whatâs New

- Using the **Custom Expression Editor**, you can now use formulas to transform data from one data
  type to another and format it accurately.

## April 12, 2022

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fixed the issue in Workday Connector in which the values in the <strong>Currency</strong> and <strong>Pay Plan</strong> fields of an <strong>Update Job Requisition</strong> action were not ordered alphabetically.</p></td><td><p>W-10663753</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## April 4, 2022

### Whatâs New

- You can now connect a new system: Google Calendar.

  See [the product documentation](ms_composer_reference) for details about each connector.

## March 31, 2022

### Whatâs New

- You can now connect a new system: Gmail.

  See [the product documentation](ms_composer_reference) for details about each connector.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Updated error messages in Google Sheets Connector for empty list fields in the <strong>Create Row</strong> action.</p></td><td><p>W-10574328</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fixed the issue in Google Sheets Connector in which columns without headers were incorrectly assigned the header of the next column with a header.</p></td><td><p>W-10899109</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## March 28, 2022

### Whatâs New

- The following objects are now available for NetSuite Connector:
  - Vendor
  - Vendor Bill
  - Purchase Order

## March 23, 2022

### Whatâs New

- The **On New or Update Invoice** trigger and the **Get Invoices** action are now available for
  Xero Connector.

## March 22, 2022

### Whatâs New

- You can now use the **Custom Expressions Editor** to build expressions and apply functions in
  Composer. This release supports basic math operators and basic functions such as string, date, and
  number.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fixed the issue in Jira Connector in which flow triggers were not activated if the field values contained spaces.</p></td><td><p>W-10830185, W-10824255</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## March 14, 2022

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fixed the issue in Salesforce Connector in which objects containing boolean fields without set default values did not return metadata.</p></td><td><p>W-10808550</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## March 9, 2022

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fixed the issue in Salesforce Connector in which remaining access tokens were not refreshed in the <strong>Session Management</strong> page after deleting an access token.</p></td><td><p>W-10718676</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## March 2, 2022

### Whatâs New

- The **Get Organization by ID** action is now available for Zendesk Connector. This action returns
  an organization based on specified ID search criteria.

## February 21, 2022

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fixed the issue in NetSuite Connector in which triggers with the <strong>Type of Record</strong> field set to <code>NON_INVENTORY_SALE_ITEM</code> returned data from other record types.</p></td><td><p>W-10674134</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## February 7, 2022

### Whatâs New

- In Stripe Connector, in the **Create Product** action, the **Images** field is now available. This
  field enables you to add the URL of to up to eight images that are then attached to the action.
- In the QuickBooks Connector, the following fields are now available in the **Create Invoice**
  action:
  - **Transaction Tax Code**
  - **Item Tax Code**

## January 27, 2022

### Whatâs New

- The Date Time picker is now available. You can either **Pick from Calendar** or **Search Data
  Pills** in fields with the data type `date` or `time`. The date picker uses the time zone set for
  your Composer organization settings by default.
- The **Flow Details** page now shows the **Created on** and **Last Modified** dates in the time
  zone set for Composer organiation.
- When an error occurs, if there is additional information about the error, you can click the **More
  details** link in the error message. In the **Error** window, you can troubleshoot the issue or
  copy the **Trace ID** to provide to support. You can also click the **Contact MuleSoft Support
  Team** button to contact support directly.

## January 25, 2022

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>The <strong>Update row</strong> action in Google Sheets Connector did not accept a data pill as an input for the <strong>Row number</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>COCN-910</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## January 19, 2022

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Upgraded library dependencies.</p></td><td><p>COCN-873</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## January 13, 2022

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Upgraded library dependencies.</p></td><td><p>COCN-894</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## January 7, 2022

### Whatâs New

- MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce now supports the `list of strings` data pill. You can now type
  characters, dates, booleans, or numbers into a field and a list of matching strings is returned in
  a dropdown.

## January 5, 2022

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fixed the issue in which NetSuite Connectorâs metadata resolution fails and produces an empty error message. The error no longer occurs.</p></td><td><p>COCN-875</p></td></tr></tbody></table>
---

# Release Notes for MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce - 2023

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Use MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce to build connected experiences faster. You donât need to wait
for development resources to unlock data, integrate apps, and automate business processes that
create real customer value.

New versions are automatically available once they are released; you donât need to perform any tasks
to move to the new version.

See
[Known Issues and Limitations for MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce](ms_composer_release_notes_ki)
for a list of known issues and limitations by date.

## December 14, 2023

### Whatâs New

- For triggers in the following connectors, the **Polling Frequency** field is now required:
  - MuleSoft RPA
  - Sage Intacct
  - SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  - ServiceNow
  - Slack
  - Snowflake
  - Zendesk

    This field enables you to select how often Composer makes a request to the underling system API
    to look for changes to records in active flows.

## December 6, 2023

### Whatâs New

- For triggers in the following connectors, the **Polling Frequency** field is now required:
  - Asana
  - Box
  - Gmail
  - Google Big Query
  - Google Sheets
  - Hubspot
  - Jira
  - Marketo
  - Netsuite

    This field enables you to select how often Composer makes a request to the underling system API
    to look for changes to records in active flows.

## December 1, 2023

### Whatâs New

- For triggers in QuickBooks Online Connector and Stripe Connector, the **Polling Frequency** field
  is now required. This field enables you to select how often Composer makes a request to the
  underling system API to look for changes to records in active flows.

## November 15, 2023

### Whatâs New

- Salesforce Connector now supports Salesforce API v59.0.

## November 14, 2023

### Whatâs New

- For triggers in Salesforce Connector, the **Polling Frequency** field is now required. This field
  enables you to select how often Composer makes a request to the underling system API to look for
  changes to records in active flows.

## November 13, 2023

### Whatâs New

- You can now search for templates (by template name, connector name, or template description) and
  filter templates (by use case category or connector).

## October 31, 2023

### Whatâs New

- When selecting outputs for an Invocable Flow, the `List<Primitive>` and `List<Object>` data types
  are now available. Additionally, you can append or prepend items to your List variable using
  dynamic or static list mappings.

## October 19, 2023

### Whatâs New

- When defining a variable, the `List<Primitive>` and `List<Object>` data types are now available.
  Additionally, you can append or prepend items to your `List` variable using dynamic or static list
  mappings.
- You can now define a primitive list by mapping a `List<Object>` data pill to a primitive list
  value within your flow.
- You now have the option to change connections for all steps (at the flow level) instead of
  individually changing connections for each step.

## October 13, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Implemented backend changes in HubSpot Connector to support Mule Runtime 4.5.0.</p></td><td><p>W-14224733</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## October 5, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>SAP S/4HANA Cloud Connector no longer throws a null error when retrieving metadata for some entity types.</p></td><td><p>W-13568493, W-14194111</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## October 4, 2023

### Whatâs New

- Using typeahead, you can now create a list of primitive data types statically by using \[ \] in
  the **Custom Expression Editor**, for example, \[name1@gmail.com, name2@gmail.com\].
- You can now use a statically-built list as an input parameter in the `joinBy` function.

## October 3, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>If the access token fails to refresh in a flow, you are now informed about the issue. You can now see the error in <strong>Run History</strong> and in an email notification (if opted-in). You must restart the flow to benefit from this fixed issue.</p></td><td><p>W-13628406</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## September 21, 2023

### Whatâs New

- Picklist Mapping is now enabled for all string fields. Using Picklist Mapping, you can map data
  from one application with the corresponding data needed by another application. This enables you
  to connect two applications that have different values for a similar field and to align the values
  in those fields across applications. You can determine how the possible values of an output field
  (data pill) map to the possible values of an input field. You can reuse the mapping across flows.
- You can now specify output fields in your invocable Composer flow so that these output fields are
  returned in the response payload to the system that triggers the invocable Composer flow.

## August 25, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Jira Connector, file transfer from Jira to Salesforce now works as intended.</p></td><td><p>W-13632302</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## August 17, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In NetSuite Connector, the <code>Unable to retrieve custom Fields from Netsuite for customizationId customSegment</code> metadata error is no longer thrown when using a NetSuite org that does not have custom segments enabled.</p></td><td><p>W-13896697</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## August 16, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Salesforce Connector now displays errors when object or field permissions are removed.</p></td><td><p>W-13625323</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## August 7, 2023

### Whatâs New

- In the **Custom Expression Editor**, you can now update any `List<Primitive>` (for example,
  `List<String>` or `List<Number>`) and manually build a list using commas and the typeahead
  feature. You cannot build the list in-line; however, you can select an existing `List` data pill
  of that data type to build your list.
- The following `List` functions are now available for the **Custom Expression Editor**:
  - `joinBy`: Merges a list into a single string value and uses the provided string as a separator
    between each item in the list.
  - `splitBy`: Splits a string into a list of strings based on a separating string that matches part
    of the input string.

## July 31, 2023

### Whatâs New

- You can now invoke a Composer flow from external systems, such as Flow Builder, Flow
  Orchestration, another Composer flow, an Apex callout, or a cURL command. This feature enables
  deeper interoperability and integrated capabilities across Salesforce. For more information, refer
  to [Invoke a Composer Flow](ms_composer_invocable_flows).

## July 27, 2023

### Whatâs New

- To enhance connectivity, you can now use OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials to create a connection in
  Composer to MuleSoft RPA Connector.

## July 14, 2023

### Whatâs New

- In Stripe Connector, the **On New Refund** trigger now outputs the following fields:
  - **Balance Transaction**
  - **Payment Intent**
  - **Charge ID**

## July 12, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In NetSuite Connector, design metadata errors are now shown to the user.</p></td><td><p>W-13587716</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## July 10, 2023

### Whatâs New

- To improve discoverability, you can now refresh the value providers and other fields in a step
  within your flow by clicking the refresh button on the step header. This button appears when you
  hover above the refresh button for the step.

## July 3, 2023

### Whatâs New

- The following enhancements appear on the **Flow Details** page:
  - The **Last Triggered** field now appears in the heading of the **Flow Details** page. This field
    displays the last time that a flow was triggered. Refreshing the **Flow Details** page also
    refreshes the value in the **Last Triggered** field.
  - The **Reference ID** field now appears in the **Run History** section. For Event triggers, the
    value in this field is the corresponding **Record ID**. For events triggered by the Scheduler, a
    unique **Execution ID** value is created for the run instance.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In RPA Connector, the <strong>RPA Process Execution Completed</strong> trigger no longer fails every few seconds with an <code>UNKNOWN ERROR</code> message.</p></td><td><p>W-13697777</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## June 28, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In RPA Connector, if a connection exception occurs, the <strong>RPA Process Execution Completed</strong> trigger now reconnects.</p></td><td><p>W-13280827</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## June 27, 2023

### Whatâs New

- You can now define a variable as temporary storage in your flow and use it as a data pill.
  Defining a variable enables you to complete certain use cases like aggregating calculations and
  storing values. You define variables locally within a flow and use them anywhere within that flow.

## June 26, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Jira Connector, custom fields are now visible in projects with no issues created.</p></td><td><p>W-13498207</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## June 21, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Salesforce Connector now properly displays required DateTime fields.</p></td><td><p>W-13606652</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## June 20, 2023

### Whatâs New

- The **Custom Expression Editor** now supports typeahead. You can now type math operators in the
  **Custom Expression Editor** and select them using the Enter key, Tab key, or mouse with
  typeahead. You no longer need to toggle the operator tab and manually select the math operator
  from the list.

## June 15, 2023

### Whatâs New

- The Error Handler is now generally available. You can use this feature to watch for errors in
  connection or logic in specific steps of your flow and then designate logic to handle the errors
  that may occur.

## June 13, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Salesforce Connector, the <strong>Get records</strong> action no longer throws an error when the value of a condition contains an apostrophe.</p></td><td><p>W-13192901</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## June 8, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In NetSuite Connector, custom fields now appear as intended.</p></td><td><p>W-13552619</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## June 6, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>The <strong>fromStringToDateTime</strong> function now preserves the timezone as intended. Active flows receive this change when they are deactivated. Inactive flows show an error and you must manually change the format parameter of the formula to <code>uuuu-MM-ddâTâHH:mm:ssz</code>.</p></td><td><p>W-13488568</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## June 3, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In NetSuite Connector, custom fields now appear as intended for the RETURN_AUTHORIZATION record type.</p></td><td><p>W-13484699</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## May 31, 2023

### Whatâs New

- You can now connect to a new system: SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

  See [the product documentation](ms_composer_reference) for details about each connector.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>QuickBooks Connector now includes an enhanced implementation for input parameters, such as better mapping when input parameters are not specified or are <code>null</code>.</p></td><td><p>W-13280661</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>In NetSuite Connector, custom fields now appear as intended.</p></td><td><p>W-13222742, W-13304252, W-13481333</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## May 25, 2023

### Whatâs New

- When adding a trigger or an action, you can now use the new search bar in the **System Action**
  and **System Event** windows to enter the specific name of a connector or shared API to use in
  your flow.

## May 24, 2023

### Fixed Issues

## May 17, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Jira Connector, the exception message is now more clear when there is an invalid organization site URL.</p></td><td><p>W-13143023</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## May 12, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Salesforce Connector, custom metadata objects are no longer shown as metadata keys for the <strong>Create new record</strong>, <strong>Update record</strong>, and <strong>Create or update record</strong> actions as intended.</p></td><td><p>W-13182376</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## May 9, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Sage Connector now automatically reconnects when there is a session timeout.</p></td><td><p>W-13003862</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## May 8, 2023

### Whatâs New

- A new option, **Only the first record**, is now enabled for the **Get records** action in
  Salesforce and NetSuite Connectors. Using this option, you can return a single record by using a
  unique identifier in your search criteria. When selected, the **Get records** action searches for
  all records that meet the selected criteria and returns the first record in the list that meets
  the criteria.

## May 5, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>To resolve metadata load errors caused by timeouts, requests to retrieve metadata from the API in NetSuite Connector are now optimized.</p></td><td><p>W-12411817</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## May 3, 2023

### Whatâs New

- An optional new field, **Organization Site URL**, appears when creating a connection for Jira
  Connector. The **Organization Site URL** field enables you to enter the URL of the Jira
  organization containing the sandbox or production environment to which you want to connect.

## April 25, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Snowflake Connector now validates account names by checking for invalid special characters.</p></td><td><p>W-12598991</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>In NetSuite Connector, the <strong>Get Records</strong> action now correctly supports the <strong>OTHER_CHARGE_SALE_ITEM</strong> record type.</p></td><td><p>W-13001785</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## April 20, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Error messages in HTTP Connector now display more precise error types.</p></td><td><p>W-12483461</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>When you complete the <strong>Operation Values</strong> field in REST Consumer Connector, an error indicating that a value is required no longer occurs.</p></td><td><p>W-12648223</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Rest Consumer Connector now properly recognizes the values provided in the <strong>Client Secret</strong> and <strong>Client ID</strong> fields while using API Sharing.</p></td><td><p>W-12601570</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## April 17, 2023

### Whatâs New

- The Error Handler (Beta) is now available. You can use this feature to watch for errors in
  connection or logic in specific steps of your flow and then designate logic to handle the errors
  that may occur.

## April 12, 2023

### Whatâs New

- The **Custom Expression Editor** now supports the following `string` functions:
  - `emptyString`
  - `substring`

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In QuickBooks Online Connector, an exception with an unknown description no longer occurs when custom fields are not specified.</p></td><td><p>W-12724854</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## April 11, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Xero Connector, fixed the issue in which a flow using the <strong>Create or Update Invoice</strong> object was not triggered when testing.</p></td><td><p>W-12670939</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## April 4, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Jira Connector, the <strong>Get issues</strong> action now works as intended when the <strong>Type</strong> field contains a whitespace.</p></td><td><p>W-12760964</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## April 3, 2023

### Whatâs New

- The following actions are now available for Slack Connector:
  - **Add users to channel**
  - **Create channel**
  - **Send direct message to multiple users**

## March 28, 2023

### Whatâs New

- If a connector update or bug fix may cause interruptions to your flows, you now receive an email
  describing these updates and outlining which flows you should stop and reactivate to avoid flow
  failures.

## March 24, 2023

### Whatâs New

- In NetSuite Connector, the log on time metadata is now registered to each record.
- In NetSuite Connector, the metadata for the **File** object is now available for the **Create
  Actions** record.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In NetSuite Connector, the <strong>Get Records</strong> action no longer returns the incorrect record for the <strong>LOT_NUMBERED_ASSEMBLY_ITEM</strong> record type.</p></td><td><p>W-12473442</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>In NetSuite Connector, the <strong>USER_ERROR: Invalid log in attempt</strong> error no longer occurs.</p></td><td><p>W-12619973</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>In NetSuite Connector, the <strong>Name</strong> field no longer appears in write operations.</p></td><td><p>W-12428757</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>In NetSuite Connector, the <strong>Type</strong> dropdown in the <strong>Get Records</strong> action no longer causes a <strong>NETSUITE_SOAP_FAULT</strong> error.</p></td><td><p>W-12572300</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>The NetSuite Connector no longer fails to parse the <strong>com.sun.xml.internal.stream.events.EndElementEvent</strong> error response.</p></td><td><p>W-12573323</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## March 23, 2023

### Whatâs New

- Previously, boolean fields that supported expressions rendered as either text fields or as
  picklists. All boolean fields that support expressions now render as a hybrid field that supports
  both text fields and picklists. Additionally, if you manually enter `true` or `false` into a text
  field, the field automatically selects the corresponding dropdown value regardless of letter case.
- When hovering over data type icons for fields, informational tooltips now appear.

## March 22, 2023

### Whatâs New

- You can now connect to a new system: Google BigQuery.

  See [the product documentation](ms_composer_reference) for details about each connector.

## March 14, 2023

### Whatâs New

- MuleSoft Anypoint Platform customers can now share externally available APIs managed in Anypoint
  Platform with business users for consumption in a MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce flow. This
  enhancement creates integrations that span beyond out-of-the-box connectors.
- Organizations that use both MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce and Anypoint Platform can now connect
  the two organizations through Access Management.

## March 13, 2023

### Whatâs New

- You can now import a flow as a JSON file by clicking the wrench icon and selecting **Import**. The
  import flows feature is in open beta.

## March 9, 2023

### Whatâs New

- In Salesforce Connector, the **Run History** page now displays detailed error log information to
  enable effective troubleshooting. Error logs now include the date and time on which the error
  occurred, an explanation of the error, and the location within the flow where the error occurred.

## March 2, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fixed the issue in Jira Connector that occurred when there was a discrepancy between data providers containing the field <code>project</code> in their parameters.</p></td><td><p>W-12270412</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## February 27, 2023

### Whatâs New

- If a Composer connector supports a default value for a field, that value now appears and is
  selected in the Composer UI; however, the user can choose to override the default value.

## February 21, 2023

### Whatâs New

- The **Composer Administrator** permission set is now available. After the installation of
  Composer, the system administrator can assign the following permissions to users:
  - The **Composer Administrator** has access to the **Settings** page and can manage email
    preferences and API Sharing.
  - The **Composer User** has general access to Composer functionality such as building and managing
    flows.

- The **Settings** page is now available for those with the **Composer Administrator** permission.
  On this page, you can view and edit email notification settings.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fixed the possible null pointer exception that sometimes occurred when custom fields were not specified.</p></td><td><p>W-12572592</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## February 17, 2023

### Whatâs New

- In Gmail Connector, the **On New Email** trigger and the **Send email** action now support
  attachments.
- The **Get Attachments** action is now available for Gmail Connector.

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Zendesk Connector, the external ID value is now represented as a string instead of a number as per the Zendesk API.</p></td><td><p>W-12360287</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## February 15, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Tableau Connector, the native libraries were upgraded to address reported security vulnerabilities.</p></td><td><p>W-11453219</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## February 13, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In HTTP Connector, the correct payload is now sent when a top-level array is defined in the API specification endpoint.</p></td><td><p>W-12373559</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## February 8, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Now, when you open a flow, all steps aside from <strong>If/Else Blocks</strong> and <strong>For Each Loops</strong> are collapsed to provide a navigable outline of the flow.</p></td><td><p>W-12515426</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## February 7, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Rest Consumer Connector, the correct payload is now sent when a top-level array is defined in the API specification endpoint.</p></td><td><p>W-12373559</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## February 6, 2023

### Whatâs New

- You can now connect to a new system: Sage Intacct.

  See [the product documentation](ms_composer_reference) for details about each connector.

## February 3, 2023

### Whatâs New

- The following enhancements have been made for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central Connector:
  - The **Create Sales Order** action now enables users to create a list of sales order lines within
    the sales order object.
  - The **Update Sales Order** action now enables users to update a list of sales order lines in
    addition to the sales order object.
  - The **Get Sales Orders** action now enables users to retrieve sales order lines corresponding to
    a filtered list of sales orders.
  - The **On New or Updated Sales Order** trigger now checks for new or updated sales order lines in
    addition to new or updated sales orders.
  - If you use a **Display Name** for the company, the **Display Name** is now populated in the
    **Company ID** dropdown instead of the **Company Name**.

## February 1, 2023

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Jira Connector, the metadata output for the <code>labels</code> property now returns an array of strings instead of an array of empty objects.</p></td><td><p>W-11899482</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>In Jira Connector, the <strong>Retrieve attachment</strong> action now shows the detailed error message returned by the Jira endpoint.</p></td><td><p>W-12282452</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## January 30, 2023

### Whatâs New

- Quickbooks Online Connector now supports custom fields for actions and triggers.
- The following actions are now available for Quickbooks Online Connector:
  - **Create Estimate**
  - **Get Estimate**
  - **Update Estimate**

- The following triggers are now available for Quickbooks Online Connector:
  - **On New Estimate**
  - **On New or Updated Estimate**

  action:
  - Department
  - Locations
  - Price Level
  - Subsidiary

  - CLASSIFICATION
  - GIFT_CERTIFICATE_ITEM
  - INVENTORY_ADJUSTMENT
  - ITEM_RECEIPT

  - TRANSFER_ORDER

  - ITEM_GROUP
  - KIT_ITEM

### Whatâs New

# Release Notes for MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce - 2024

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Use MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce to build connected experiences faster. You donât need to wait
for development resources to unlock data, integrate apps, and automate business processes that
create real customer value.

New versions are automatically available once they are released; you donât need to perform any tasks
to move to the new version.

See
[Known Issues and Limitations for MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce](ms_composer_release_notes_ki)
for a list of known issues and limitations by date.

## July 9, 2024

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Workday Connector, testing a flow that has an error handler containing a Workday connection no longer gets stuck in the processing status and incorrectly fails with the <code>Test failed because of external errors. Please try again.</code> error.</p></td><td><p>W-15239656</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## May 17, 2024

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>NetSuite Connector no longer fails to retrieve custom values in an Inventory record.</p></td><td><p>W-15138793</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>In NetSuite Connector, the <strong>Get Records</strong> action now returns the correct <code>Internal ID</code> for values in fields containing multiple picklists.</p></td><td><p>W-15194262</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## March 21, 2024

### Whatâs New

- You can now view descriptions of connector actions and triggers directly under their respective
  names.

## March 15, 2024

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>After an access token expires, Salesforce Marketing Cloud Connector now properly refreshes the access token.</p></td><td><p>W-14979479</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## March 11, 2024

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Jira Connector, you can now create new connections in Composer as intended.</p></td><td><p>W-15070963</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## February 28, 2024

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central Connector no longer incorrectly fails with an <code>Invalid Connection</code> error when creating a connection.</p></td><td><p>W-14824218</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## January 9, 2024

### Whatâs New

- For triggers in the following connectors, the **Polling Frequency** field is now required:
  - Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central
  - Workday
  - Xero

    This field enables you to select how often Composer makes a request to the underling system API
    to look for changes to records in active flows.
---

for development resources to unlock data, integrate apps, and automate business processes that
create real customer value.

New versions are automatically available once they are released; you donât need to perform any tasks
to move to the new version.

[Known Issues and Limitations for MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce](ms_composer_release_notes_ki)
for a list of known issues and limitations by date.

# Release Notes for MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce - 2026

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Use MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce to build connected experiences faster. You donât need to wait
for development resources to unlock data, integrate apps, and automate business processes that
create real customer value.

New versions are automatically available once they are released; you donât need to perform any tasks
to move to the new version.

See
[Known Issues and Limitations for MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce](ms_composer_release_notes_ki)
for a list of known issues and limitations by date.

## May 28, 2026

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>To prevent excessive errors from abandoned processes and reduce the risk of service incidents, MuleSoft Composer automatically deactivates flows that experience continuous failures and alerts affected users. Deactivation occurs only when a flow meets both of these conditions:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>100 or more consecutive errors</p></li><li><p>10 or more days of continuous failure</p></li></ul></div><div><p>You can reactivate a deactivated flow at any time.</p></div></div></td><td><p>W-22571502</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## March 19, 2026

### Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Jira Connector now correctly updates the watermark for the triggers, and captures all subsequent new and updated issues.</p></td><td><p>W-20186115</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>In Jira Connector, all triggers now correctly update the watermark when multiple records share the same minute-level timestamp. The connector now uses cursor-based pagination to retrieve all records for a specific watermark before updating the watermark value. This prevents duplicate record retrieval and resolves issues where flows enter an indefinite waiting state.</p></td><td><p>W-21090825</p></td></tr></tbody></table>
---

# Known Issues and Limitations for MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following list displays known issues and limitations for MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce. The
list is sorted by the date in which the known issue or limitation appeared.

## September 12, 2025

### Composer Platform

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Composerâs functionality may be subject to various limitations related to the external systems and their respective limits used in data retrieval. Composer is primarily intended for processing simpler and more lightweight datasets. It is important to understand that there is no single, rigid limit and that specific thresholds vary depending on each individual use case. For example, the following limits apply to <a href="https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.apexcode.meta/apexcode/apex_gov_limits.htm">Salesforce Connector</a>.</p></td><td><p>For use cases requiring greater control, advanced logic, or larger datasets, users should consider <a href="../anypoint-code-builder/">Anypoint Code Builder</a>.</p></td><td><p>W-19176552</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## August 19, 2024

### Salesforce

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Salesforce Connector, the <code>SALESFORCE:CONNECTIVITY Failed to get next element</code> error is sometimes thrown when processing the next element of the loop.</p></td><td><p>Rerun the flow to catch the missed events. For more information, refer to the <a href="https://help.salesforce.com/s/issue?language=en_US&amp;id=a028c00000zLDsc">known issue</a>.</p></td><td><p>W-16418279</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## May 7, 2024

### NetSuite

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>When selecting a record type, if there is a large amount of metadata, the metadata resolver cannot parse the data within the three-minute time limit required by Composer and the <code>Netsuite: Retrieving metadata for the step failed</code> error sometimes occurs.</p></td><td><p>For more information, see the <a href="https://help.salesforce.com/s/issue?language=en_US&amp;id=a028c00000zHtne">knowledge article</a>.</p></td><td><p>W-15586159</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## October 31, 2023

### Composer Platform

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>When testing an Invocable Flow, if the value in the output fields was generated by a custom expression or if the value is either a <code>List&lt;Object&gt;</code> or a <code>List&lt;Primitive&gt;</code> data type, then the flowâs final output step does not display the test output data. Output fields that refer to data pills or variables display test output as expected.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## May 31, 2023

### SAP S/4HANA Cloud

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>When adding an action or a trigger, the <strong>Service</strong> field dropdown menu might appear empty, which prevents completing the connector configuration.</p><p>Similarly, during configuration, the <code>Retrieving metadata for the step failed</code> error might occur.</p></td><td><p>This is an intermittent issue that should resolve after a brief period of time.</p></td><td><p>W-13527982</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## March 22, 2023

### Google BigQuery

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Input parameter values of String or date data types must be provided between single quotes using the expression editor.</p></td><td><p>W-12696210</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## August 3, 2022

### RPA

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>If connecting to a different RPA than one for which that the existing flow was built, the current RPA process is not automatically mapped to the processes in the new org.</p></td><td><div><div><p>Complete the following steps:</p></div><div><ol><li><p>Delete the step in the flow.</p></li><li><p>Re-add the step in the flow.</p></li><li><p>Refresh the process list.</p></li><li><p>Select the new process.</p></li></ol></div></div></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>When getting a JSON from RPA, you can pass a list of objects to a connector supporting lists. For example, you can pass the list of order line items to NetSuite.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>None.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>When getting a JSON from RPA, you cannot use primitive values of a list when iterating the list. For example, a list of strings can be iterated, but you cannot do anything with the resulting values.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## July 13, 2022

### Composer Platform

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>When deleting a connection, Composer ensures that there are no flows using that connection. If there are flows using that connection, they appear in a list in the <strong>Delete Connection</strong> window. However, in rare cases, flows created or edited before July 12, 2022 might not appear in the list.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## April 29, 2022

### Workday

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In the <strong>Invoice</strong> object, all fields containing the data type <code>number</code> for amounts are sent as strings.</p></td><td><p>Using the Custom Expression Editor, you can use the typecasting function, <code>fromStringToNumber</code>, to change the field type from <code>string</code> to <code>number</code>. For example:<br></p><p><code>Unit Cost=fromStringToNumber(Sales Price)</code><br></p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## April 28, 2022

### HTTP

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>After changing the text in the <strong>Example Request</strong> field, the <strong>HTTP Request</strong> fields do not clear.</p></td><td><p>Rebuild the action.</p></td><td><p>W-11052290</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Some API endpoints expect a list as input. Composer only supports creating one list from another.</p></td><td><p>Add a previous step that returns a list to your flow and use it as a basis for the list required in the HTTP request.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>When testing a connection, the <strong>Request Body</strong> field appears even if the <strong>Request Method</strong> field is set to <code>GET</code>.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## January 5, 2022

### NetSuite

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>An error occurs in Composer when testing your flow.</p></td><td><div><div><p>In your Chrome browser, click <strong>Developer Tools</strong> and then click <strong>Network</strong>. In the <strong>Network</strong> tab in Chrome, test your flow again. If you encounter the <code>INSUFFICIENT PERMISSIONS</code> error, complete the following steps:<br></p></div><div><ol><li><p>In NetSuite, access your role configuration.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Permissions</strong> and then click <strong>Setup</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Ensure that the following permissions are set to <strong>Full</strong>:</p><div><ul><li><p>Control SuiteScript and Workflow Triggers in Web Service Request</p></li><li><p>SOAP Web Service</p></li><li><p>User Access Tokens</p></li><li><p>Custom Body Fields</p></li><li><p>Custom Column Fields</p></li><li><p>Custom Entity</p></li><li><p>Custom Item Fields</p></li><li><p>Custom Item Number Fields</p></li><li><p>Custom Lists</p></li><li><p>Custom Record Types</p></li><li><p>Custom Transaction Fields</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>Reestablish the NetSuite connection. If the error continues to appear, contact Support.</p></li></ol></div></div></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## July 1, 2021

### Jira

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>The following standard fields are not supported at this time:<br></p></div><div><ul><li><p><strong>Affects version</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>Fix version</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>Environment</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>Component</strong></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Custom fields containing the following list components fail to render:<br></p></div><div><ul><li><p><strong>Text field (multi-line)</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>Labels</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>Checkboxes</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>Radio Buttons</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>Select list (multiple-choice)</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>Select list (cascading)</strong></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>In the <strong>Status</strong> field, the <strong>In Progress</strong> value sometimes appears twice.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>After creating a case in Jira, the <strong>Update issue</strong> action does not update the value in the <strong>Reporter</strong> field. Additionally, the value in this field must be blank for the <strong>Update issue</strong> action to succeed.</p></td><td><p>To update the value in the <strong>Reporter</strong> field, you can manually update the case record in Jira.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom fields fail to appear.</p></td><td><p>Reset your Jira connection by deleting the connection and re-authenticating your Jira account.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>When you create an issue, you may encounter a 400 error in the output metadata.</p></td><td><p>Disregard this error.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Known Issue</th><th>Workaround</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>If you donât see the <strong>New Flow</strong> button on the <strong>Recently Viewed</strong> list page, switch to the <strong>All</strong> page.</p></td><td><p>None.</p></td><td><p>CAPP-1625</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>If you see a blank area instead of the <strong>Home</strong> page contents when you first log in to Composer, clear your browser cookies and reload.</p></td><td><p>None.</p></td><td><p>CAPP-2051</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>You canât access the <strong>Add Connection</strong> button from a trigger or action once it has been created.</p></td><td><div><div><p>To change a connection to a new connection of the same system type:<br></p></div><div><ol><li><p>Add an action to the end of your flow, which displays the <strong>Add Connection</strong> button.</p></li><li><p>Create your new connection.</p></li><li><p>Delete the action you added.</p></li><li><p>In the trigger or action where you want to change to the new connection, click the change icon (three dots).</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Change Connection</strong>. The new connection is listed.</p></li></ol></div></div></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>After you create a trigger, you canât delete that step.</p></td><td><div><div><p>If you want to change the data source, for example switching from Salesforce to Workday, create a new flow.</p></div><div><p>If you want to change the instance of the same data source, but the connection for that source doesnât exist yet:<br></p></div><div><ol><li><p>Add an action to the end of your flow. In the action, create the connection you need.</p></li><li><p>Delete the action.</p></li><li><p>Open the trigger and use <strong>Change Connection</strong> to change to the new connection.</p></li></ol></div></div></td><td><p>CAPP-1550</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>As part of the Salesforce action <strong>Get records</strong>, you canât query based on a Date field.</p></td><td><p>None.</p></td><td><p>CPLAT-1126</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

# Rest Consumer Connector Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Rest Consumer Connector connects externally available APIs managed in Anypoint that are not
currently supported through dedicated connectors. Use Rest Consumer Connector to consume these APIs
in a Composer flow to create integrations that span beyond out-of-the-box connectors.

Rest Consumer Connector does not automatically appear in the list of connectors to use in your flow.
To use Rest Consumer Connector, you must first connect your Composer org to your Anypoint org. For
more information, refer to
[Connect Composer to Anypoint Platform](ms_composer_prerequisites#connect-composer-to-anypoint-platform)
and [API Sharing](ms_composer_prerequisites#api-sharing).

## Connections

To connect to a system, whether it is a source of data or the target where you are sending data, you
must create a connection with that systemâs required credentials. You typically connect to different
systems within a flow and can reuse each connection every time you need to connect.

This system requires the following credential information for its connections:

Connection Display Name

Enter a connection name that will help you remember the details of this connection. You can reuse
connections multiple times in a single flow, and in more than one flow, and the credentials are
hidden after you create the connection. Therefore, itâs helpful to provide a name that easily
identifies this connection from others.

Only you can see the connection. Other users in your Composer app must create their own connections.

Authorization Type

The following authorization types are available:

- **API Key**  
  The API Key connects to applications without explicitly defining a user. You must add this key to
  all requests, and the cloud application identifies the origin of the request and completes the
  authorization. Provide a value for the following fields:
  - **Base URL**
  - **Name**
  - **Value**
  - **Sent As**: Select one of the following values:
    - **Header**: If selected, Composer adds the following header to the request: `Name: Value`.
    - **Query Parameter**: If selected, Composer adds the following query parameter to the request:
      `Name=Value`.

  - **Client ID**: This field is used for gateway authentication, which occurs before connected API
    authentication.
  - **Client Secret**: This field is used for gateway authentication, which occurs before connected
    API authentication.

- **Basic Authentication**  
  The Basic Authentication method enables an HTTP user agent to provide a username and password when
  making an HTTP request. Provide a value for the following fields:
  - **Base URL**
  - **Username**
  - **Password**
  - **Client ID**: This field is used for gateway authentication, which occurs before connected API
    authentication.
  - **Client Secret**: This field is used for gateway authentication, which occurs before connected
    API authentication.

- **Bearer Token Authorization**  
  Bearer Token Authorization is the authentication process that sends a header named `Authorization`
  with a value of `Bearer` and a token with every request. Provide a value for the following fields:
  - **Base URL**
  - **Token**
  - **Client ID**: This field is used for gateway authentication, which occurs before connected API
    authentication.
  - **Client Secret**: This field is used for gateway authentication, which occurs before connected
    API authentication.

Base URL

The default location URL for the cloud application that the connection uses.

## Actions

After you connect to a system and define the trigger or configure a scheduler, you define the
actions that the flow performs. For example, you can copy a record between different systems, such
as Workday and Salesforce. The actions you can perform depend on which system you are connected to.

This system provides the following actions:

Request

When **Request** is selected in the **Action** field, a request in Rest Consumer returns the fields
of the response payload. A value is required for the following fields:

- **Action**
- **Operation**
- **Input Fields**: You can select from a static list or use a dynamic list from a data pill based
  on the previous step.
  - Optionally, if you click **URI Params**, provide a value for the **Ticket** field.
  - Optionally, if you click **Additional Headers**, provide a value for the following fields:
    - **Key**
    - **Value**

  - Optionally, if you click **Additional Query Parameters**, provide a value for the following
    fields:
    - **Key**
    - **Value**

- **Custom Request Body**: This field is used for overriding the structure of the request body from
  the API specification.
- **Custom Response Body**: This field is used for overriding the structure of the response body
  from the API specification.

## See Also

- [Mulesoft Composer for Salesforce: Overview](ms_composer_overview)
- [MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce: Getting Started](ms_composer_prerequisites)
- [Composer Connector Reference](ms_composer_reference)
- [Knowledge Articles](https://help.salesforce.com/s/)
---

# MuleSoft RPA Connector Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[Mulesoft Robotic Process Automation (RPA)](https://docs.mulesoft.com/rpa-home/) is a software
technology that combines robotics and automation to perform repetitive tasks. RPA uses a software
bot to mimic the actions a person would take, by automating repetitive manual tasks like entering
text and updating fields through one or multiple application user interfaces.

## RPA Use Cases

You can benefit in many ways by implementing RPA into your processes, either by saving time,
reducing costs, or preventing errors caused by human input.

The most common use cases for RPA include:

- Data extraction and data entry  
  Extract data from PDF, CSV, and other file formats and send this data to other systems.
- Payroll processing  
  Automate payroll data entry and validate the information across multiple systems.
- Price comparisons  
  Automate the monitoring of input prices and competitor prices.
- Processing refunds  
  Automate the processing of customer refund requests to make them quicker.
- Customer information updates  
  Update customer information automatically across different systems that do not have APIs and that
  are accessible only through the UI.

## Invoke Short-Running RPA Processes

When invoking short-running RPA processes from Composer, invoke a single flow by choosing the **Use
response in this flow** button. The RPA execution is retrieved by the RPA step and subsequent steps
can use the RPA step datapills.

If the **Use the response in the same flow option** is enabled and the process takes longer than
three minutes to respond, the process stops and an error message appears indicating that the polling
process timed out.

## Invoke Long-Running RPA Processes

When invoking RPA processes from Composer, a two-flow Composer scenario is only required when the
RPA process takes longer than the allowed completion timeout period, which is three minutes.

For long-running processes, you can configure two flows that finish within the Composer flow timeout
period to avoid blocking other in-queue events from processing while waiting for the RPA process to
complete.

For the first flow, select **Poll response from another flow** and then configure a second RPA poll
trigger flow that listens to executions for the process invoked by the first flow. When activating
or testing the two-flow scenario, ensure that you start the second poll trigger flow first and then
start the invoke flow. This way, the poll trigger flow wonât miss events generated by the invoke
flow.

If data pills from the first RPA invoke flow are needed in the second poll trigger flow, store them
in an external database using the **RPA Execution ID** as the key. The data pills are then retrieved
by the **RPA Execution ID** when the poll trigger polls the executions from RPA.

If multiple users create invoke or poll flows for the same process, track execution IDs through the
external database. If you are not using an external database to track the process execution, all RPA
executions for the selected RPA process complete and the RPA does not filter the executions by user.

## Connections

To connect to a system, whether it is a source of data or the target where you are sending data, you
must create a connection with that systemâs required credentials. You typically connect to different
systems within a flow and can reuse each connection every time you need to connect.

This system requires the following credential information for its connections:

Connection Display Name

Enter a connection name to remember the details of this connection. You can reuse connections
multiple times in a single flow, and in more than one flow, and the credentials are hidden after you
create the connection. Provide a name that easily identifies this connection from others.

Only you can see the connection. Other users in your Composer app must create their own connections.

RPA API URL

The URL provided by the RPA Admin who is creating the processes that you want to integrate via
Composer. The **RPA API URL** is the RPA Manager URL with `/rpa/api/v1` appended to it. For example,
if your RPA Manager URL is `[https://myOrg.rpa.mulesoft.com](https://myOrg.rpa.mulesoft.com)`, then
the value in the **RPA API URL** field is
`[https://myOrg.rpa.mulesoft.com/rpa/api/v1](https://myOrg.rpa.mulesoft.com/rpa/api/v1)`.

API key

The authentication code for the user. The API key is generated in the
[RPA User Management](https://docs.mulesoft.com/rpa-manager/usermanagement-connect#create-an-api-key-for-a-user)
module.

OAuth 2.0 Authorization

You can use OAuth 2.0 Client credentials to obtain an access token from a user-defined path. Provide
a value for the following:

- **RPA API URL**: The URL provided by the RPA Admin creating the processes that you want to
  integrate with Composer. The **RPA API URL** value is the RPA Manager URL with `/rpa/api/v2`
  appended to the end. For example, if your RPA Manager URL is `https://myOrg.rpa.mulesoft.com`,
  then the value in the **RPA API URL** field is `https://myOrg.rpa.mulesoft.com/rpa/api/v2`.
- **Client ID**: The OAuth Client ID that you use to log in to MuleSoft RPA.
- **Client Secret**: The OAuth Client Secret that you use to log in to MuleSoft RPA.
- **Token URL**: The URL of the endpoint that provides the access token for MuleSoft RPA. For users
  in the United States, enter: `https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/accounts/api/v2/oauth2/token`. For
  users in the European Union, enter:
  `https://eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com/accounts/api/v2/oauth2/token`.
- **Scopes**: The OAuth scopes. Enter the following OAuth scopes for full access to the RPA API:
  - `RPA Integrator`
  - `RPA Invocable Process`

## Triggers

Each flow starts with either a _scheduler_ or a _trigger_. Schedulers are set to a default frequency
of 15 minutes. Triggers, such as "each time a new record is created", are defined by you and provide
multiple options related to the data in your system.

Triggers use a polling process to actively check for new updates from an external system. The
polling frequency is 15 seconds.

This system provides the following trigger options:

RPA Process Execution Completed

Create listeners that check the status of a specific RPA process. When the RPA process finishes the
execution, the polling trigger processes the configured activities in the Composer flow. Note that
you must select whether you want listeners for _successful_ or _failed_ executions. You cannot
receive notifications for both.

### Polling Frequency

For triggers in this connector, the **Polling Frequency** field is required. This field enables you
to select how often Composer makes a request to the underling system API to look for changes to
records in active flows. You can select a different polling frequency for each flow in your
organization. With a shorter frequency, the flow executes more often; however, a shorter frequency
consumes more [API calls](../general/automation-credits-usage-and-rates). For example, if you have
an order management flow, you might require nearly real-time updates, which can be achieved with a
15-second polling interval (which equates to 5760 API calls per day). In contrast, if your flow
updates a pricing catalog, you could poll the flow every hour (which equates to 24 API calls per
day).

## Actions

After you connect to a system and define the trigger or configure a scheduler, you define the
actions that the flow performs. For example, you can copy a record between different systems, such
as Workday and Salesforce. The actions you can perform depend on which system you are connected to.

This system provides the following actions:

Invoke RPA Process

Invokes a process in RPA. Provide a value for **RPA Process Name**. Additionally, select the
activity parameters that you want to set and set values for those parameters.

## Test the RPA Process

You can test your RPA Process instantly without invoking the process.

To test your RPA Process:

1.  In your RPA Process flow, in an action or trigger, click the gear.  
    The **Step Configurations <Action or Trigger>: Invoke RPA Process** window appears.
2.  In the window, select **Donât run the RPA Process; instead, use the sample output data** and
    then click **Save**.  
    The RPA Process is skipped and tested. The output of the process appears with sample data.

## See Also

- [Mulesoft Composer for Salesforce: Overview](ms_composer_overview)
- [MuleSoft Composer for Salesforce: Getting Started](ms_composer_prerequisites)
- [Composer Connector Reference](ms_composer_reference)
- [Knowledge Articles](https://help.salesforce.com/s/)
- [Mulesoft Robotic Process Automation (RPA)](https://docs.mulesoft.com/rpa-home/)
---

# Sage Intacct Connector Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Sage Intacct is a best-in-class enterprise resource planning solution built to create and automate
important financial processes and enable real-time visibility into business performance.

## Prerequisites

To complete the integration of Sage Intacct Connector to Composer, you must first complete the
following actions within Sage Intacct:

- [Enable Web Services subscription](https://developer.intacct.com/web-services/) in the
  **Subscription** section of Sage Intacct. The **Web Services Subscription** is required for
  Composer to interact with the Sage Intacct API.
- [Add your companyâs **Sender ID** to the **Web Services Authorizations**](https://developer.intacct.com/web-services/)
  section of Sage Intacct. The **Sender ID** identifies Composer to the Sage Intacct API. You must
  authorize the Mulesoft **Sender ID** for your Sage Intacct instance.
- [Create and assign the permissions required](https://developer.intacct.com/api/company-console/roles/)
  to connect to Sage Intacct. To assure account security, restrict the permissions to only the
  modules that you need Composer to access. In Sage Intacct:
  - If you are using role-based permissions, create a new role, assign the required permissions for
    that role, and then create a Sage Intacct Web Service Account to assign to the role.
  - If you are using subscription-based permissions, create a Web Services Account and assign
    permissions to the role in the **Subscription** section.

    Additionally, for quote to cash, enable permissions for the **Order Entry** module.

## Connections

To connect to a system, whether it is a source of data or the target where you are sending data, you
must create a connection with that systemâs required credentials. You typically connect to different
systems within a flow and can reuse each connection every time you need to connect.

This system requires the following credential information for its connections:

Connection Name

Enter a connection name that will help you remember the details of this connection. You can reuse
connections multiple times in a single flow, and in more than one flow, and the credentials are
hidden after you create the connection. Therefore, itâs helpful to provide a name that easily
identifies this connection from others.

Only you can see the connection. Other users in your Composer app must create their own connections.

Company ID

The customerâs company ID. Enter the ID of your Sage Intacct instance. For more information, see the
[Sage Intacct documentation](https://developer.intacct.com/web-services/).

User ID

The user ID that you use to log in to Sage Intacct.

User Password

The password that you use to log in to Sage Intacct.

Entity ID

The Sage Intacct entity to which you want to connect. To connect at the entity level, enter the
**Entity ID**. To connect to the top level of the organization, leave this field blank. For more
information, see the
[Sage Intacct documentation](https://developer.intacct.com/api/company-console/api-sessions/).

## Triggers

Each flow starts with either a _scheduler_ or a _trigger_. Schedulers are set to a default frequency
of 15 minutes. Triggers, such as "each time a new record is created", are defined by you and provide
multiple options related to the data in your system.

Triggers use a polling process to actively check for new updates from an external system. The
polling frequency is 15 seconds.

This system provides the following trigger options:

New Customer

Checks for a new Customer record.

New or Updated Customer

Checks for a new or updated Customer record.

New Order

Checks for a new Order record.

New or Updated Order

Checks for a new or updated Order record.

### Polling Frequency

For triggers in this connector, the **Polling Frequency** field is required. This field enables you
to select how often Composer makes a request to the underling system API to look for changes to
records in active flows. You can select a different polling frequency for each flow in your
organization. With a shorter frequency, the flow executes more often; however, a shorter frequency
consumes more [API calls](../general/automation-credits-usage-and-rates). For example, if you have
an order management flow, you might require nearly real-time updates, which can be achieved with a
15-second polling interval (which equates to 5760 API calls per day). In contrast, if your flow
updates a pricing catalog, you could poll the flow every hour (which equates to 24 API calls per
day).

## Actions

After you connect to a system and define the trigger or configure a scheduler, you define the
actions that the flow performs. For example, you can copy a record between different systems, such
as Workday and Salesforce. The actions you can perform depend on which system you are connected to.

This system provides the following actions:

Create Attachment

Creates a new Attachment record. In Sage Intacct, attachments are entities that contain files. An
attachment object can be empty or can have files. You can add files to an attachment when you create
the record or you can add attachments later. Provide a value for the following:

- **Attachment Name**
- **Folder**
- When adding files to an attachment, use a list as the source of the files, and specify a value in
  the following fields:
  - **File Binary Data**
  - **File Extension**
  - **File Name**

Update Attachment

Updates an Attachment record. When updating an attachment, you can update the attachment properties
or add new files from a list. Provide a value for **Attachment ID**.

Get Attachments

Returns a list of Attachment records. To filter the list, provide a value for **Attachment ID**,
**Attachment Name**, or **Folder Name**. To return the entire list, leave the fields blank.

Create Attachment Folder

Creates a new folder for attachments. Provide a value for **Name**.

Get Attachment Folders

Returns a list of attachment folders. To filter the list, provide a value for **Folder Name** to
filter. To return the entire list, leave the fields blank.

Create Customer

Creates a new Customer record. Provide a value for the following:

- **Customer ID**
- **Primary Contact**
- **Print As**

Update Customer

Update a Customer record. Provide a value for **ID**.

Get Customer

Returns a list of Customer records. To filter the list, provide a value for **Customer ID** or
**Customer Name**. To return the entire list, leave the fields blank.

Delete Customer

Deletes a Customer record. Provide a value for **Customer ID**.

Create Order

Creates a new Order record and returns a value for the **Key** field. Provide a value for the
following:

- **Order Type**
- **Date Created**
- **Transaction Currency**
- **Base Currency**
- **Exchange Rate Type**
- In the **Entries** > **Sotransitem (Order line items)** section, provide a value in the following:
